# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:20.04

# Install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y nginx postgresql redis-server python3 python3-pip && \
    apt-get clean

# Set up the web server (nginx)
RUN rm /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf 

# Set up the API server
WORKDIR /app
COPY . /app
RUN pip3 install -r requirements.txt

# Set up the database (PostgreSQL)
USER postgres
RUN /etc/init.d/postgresql start && \
    psql --command "CREATE USER myuser WITH SUPERUSER PASSWORD 'mypassword';" && \
    createdb -O myuser mydatabase

# Set back to root user
USER root

# Set up Redis
RUN sed -i 's/^# requirepass .*/requirepass mypassword/' /etc/redis/redis.conf

# Expose the necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start all services
CMD service postgresql start && \
    service redis-server start && \
    nginx && \
    python3 api_server.py

# Note: Ensure the file 'api_server.py' and 'nginx.conf' exists and is correctly set up for this configuration.
